---
title: "hack"
author: "Zachary Brunell"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---
```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r echo =FALSE, results= "hide", message=FALSE}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(lubridate)
library(RColorBrewer)
```
# Graphing
```{r echo =FALSE, results= TRUE, fig.width=16, fig.height=12}
production <- read.csv("Production_history_production_wells.csv", check.names  = TRUE)
# Ensure data type
production$Date <- ymd_hms(production$Date)
production$`Cumulative.Oil.Production..BBL` <- as.numeric(production$`Cumulative.Oil.Production..BBL`)

# ---- TRUE monthly rate: (month-end cumulative) - (prev month-end cumulative)
production <- production %>%
  filter(Date >= ymd("2020-01-01")) %>%
  mutate(Month = as.Date(floor_date(Date, "month"))) %>%
  arrange(Well_ID, Date) %>%
  group_by(Well_ID, Month) %>%
  slice_tail(n = 1) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  arrange(Well_ID, Month) %>%
  group_by(Well_ID) %>%
  mutate(Production_Rate = `Cumulative.Oil.Production..BBL` - lag(`Cumulative.Oil.Production..BBL`)) %>%
  ungroup()

y_upper <- quantile(production$Production_Rate, 1, na.rm = TRUE)
y_lower <- quantile(production$Production_Rate, 0.01, na.rm = TRUE)

# Split wells into chunks of 12
wells <- sort(unique(production$Well_ID))
well_chunks <- split(wells, ceiling(seq_along(wells) / 12))

groups_to_print <- c(2, 6)

# Global mean line by Month (must match x-axis!)
global_mean_line <- production %>%
  group_by(Month) %>%
  summarise(Global_Mean_Rate = mean(Production_Rate, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

for (i in groups_to_print) {

  chunk_wells <- well_chunks[[i]]
  plot_data <- production %>% filter(Well_ID %in% chunk_wells)

p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Month, y = Production_Rate, color = factor(Well_ID))) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.9, alpha = 0.85, na.rm = TRUE) +
  geom_line(
    data = global_mean_line,
    aes(x = Month, y = Global_Mean_Rate),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    linewidth = 1.4,
    color = "black",
    na.rm = TRUE
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(y_lower, y_upper)) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Paired") +
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "1 year", date_labels = "%Y") +
  labs(
    title = paste("Monthly Oil Production Rate by Well (Group", i, ")"),
    subtitle = "Black line = mean across wells in group",
    x = "Month",
    y = "Monthly Production Rate (BBL)",
    color = "Well ID"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x  = element_text(size = 14, angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    legend.text  = element_text(size = 14),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 14)
  )
  print(p)
}
```

# Filtering
```{r echo=TRUE, results="hide", warning=FALSE}
library(dplyr)

# Make sure data is sorted
production_sorted <- production %>%
  arrange(Well_ID, Date) %>%
  group_by(Well_ID) %>%
  mutate(row_number = row_number()) %>%
  ungroup()

# Find first nonzero production per well
first_nonzero <- production_sorted %>%
  group_by(Well_ID) %>%
  filter(Cumulative.Oil.Production..BBL > 0) %>%
  slice_head(n = 1) %>%
  select(Well_ID, row_number) %>%
  rename(first_nonzero_row = row_number)

# Join back and get row 48 months after first nonzero to ensure no bias
production_48_after_first_nonzero <- production_sorted %>%
  left_join(first_nonzero, by = "Well_ID") %>%
  filter(!is.na(first_nonzero_row) & row_number == first_nonzero_row + 48) %>%
  select(Well_ID, Date, Cumulative.Oil.Production..BBL) %>%
  rename(Cumulative_48_Months_After_First_Nonzero = Cumulative.Oil.Production..BBL)

top_10 = production_48_after_first_nonzero %>%
  arrange(desc(Cumulative_48_Months_After_First_Nonzero)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 10)
bottom_10 = production_48_after_first_nonzero %>%
  arrange((Cumulative_48_Months_After_First_Nonzero)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 10)
post = read.csv("output.csv") # File after imputation

post_10 = post %>%
  # Filter wells in top or bottom
  filter(Well_ID %in% top_10$Well_ID | Well_ID %in% bottom_10$Well_ID) %>%
  # Create a single category column
  mutate(Category = case_when(
    Well_ID %in% top_10$Well_ID ~ "Top",
    Well_ID %in% bottom_10$Well_ID ~ "Bottom"
  )) %>%
  # Group by Well_ID and Category
  group_by(Well_ID, Category) %>%
  # Calculate median for all numeric columns
  summarise(across(where(is.numeric), median, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")%>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~round(.x, 2))) %>%
  select(-X.1)
post_10
# View the result
write.csv(post_10, "top10bottom10.csv")
```

# Choose predictors
# SMD Table
```{r echo = 'FALSE', warning=FALSE}
num_cols = names(post_10)[sapply(post_10, is.numeric)]
exclude = c("Well_ID")
predictors = setdiff(num_cols, exclude)

summ_list <- list()

for (p in predictors) {

  # numeric vectors for the predictor p, split by Category
  x_top <- post_10 %>% filter(Category == "Top") %>% pull(!!p)
  x_bot <- post_10 %>% filter(Category == "Bottom") %>% pull(!!p)

  x_top <- as.numeric(x_top)
  x_bot <- as.numeric(x_bot)

  x_top <- x_top[!is.na(x_top)]
  x_bot <- x_bot[!is.na(x_bot)]

  m_top <- median(x_top)
  m_bot <- median(x_bot)

  summ_list[[length(summ_list) + 1]] <- data.frame(
    predictor = p,
    average_top = m_top,
    average_bottom = m_bot,
    diff = m_top - m_bot
  )
}

summary_df <- do.call(rbind, summ_list)
summary_df

```
# Heatmap
```{r echo = FALSE, warning=FALSE}
heat_data <- post_10 %>%
  select(where(is.numeric)) %>%
  select(-Well_ID)

# scale features
heat_scaled <- scale(heat_data)

row_order <- order(post_10$Category)
heat_scaled <- heat_scaled[row_order, ]

row_labels <- post_10$Category[row_order]
heatmap(
  heat_scaled,
  Rowv = NA,
  Colv = NA,
  scale = "none",
  labRow = row_labels,
  margins = c(6, 8),
  col = colorRampPalette(c("red", "white", "blue"))(100)
)

library(corrplot)

X <- post_10 %>%
  select(all_of(setdiff(predictors, "Z"))) %>%
  scale()   # correlation assumes standardized scale

cor_mat <- cor(X, use = "pairwise.complete.obs")

corrplot(
  cor_mat,
  method = "color",
  type = "upper",
  tl.cex = 0.7,
  tl.col = "black"
)

```
